title: Global Frost Events Benchmark Dataset
text:
This repository contains a curated collection of documented frost events across the globe.
The primary purpose of this dataset is to support the development, evaluation, and validation of frost detection algorithms, particularly those based on climate reanalysis data (e.g. ERA5) and crop models.
This dataset is not intended to be exhaustive. Instead, it provides a set of high-confidence, representative frost events across different regions, climates, and cropping systems.

title: Global Frost Events Benchmark Dataset
text:
Motivation
Frost remains a major constraint to agricultural production worldwide.
However, there is currently no standardised global dataset of frost events available for:

validating frost detection methods
comparing modelling approaches
benchmarking climate-based risk metrics

This repository aims to fill that gap by providing a transparent, reproducible benchmark dataset.

title: Global Frost Events Benchmark Dataset
text:
Contribution
Contributions are welcome.
You can: - add new events - improve metadata - refine spatial/temporal definitions
Please ensure that all new entries: - are supported by a credible source - follow the existing schema

title: Global Frost Events Benchmark Dataset
text:
Status
🚧 Under active development as part of ongoing research on global frost risk and detection methods.
This dataset is currently used to support: - algorithm development - model validation - methodological research
It may be formally released as a standalone dataset paper in the future.

title: South Australia Spring Frosts (2024)
text: Widespread frost events occurred across major grain-producing regions of South Australia during September and early October 2024. The most severe impacts were reported on the Eyre Peninsula and in the Mid North.

title: South Australia Spring Frosts (2024)
text: Widespread frost events occurred across major grain-producing regions of South Australia during September and early October 2024. The most severe impacts were reported on the Eyre Peninsula and in the Mid North.

title: South Australia Spring Frosts (2024)
text:
Meteorological context
The frost events developed during a very dry growing season, with many regions experiencing well below average rainfall. Multiple frost events occurred over a short period, including at least five days below 0°C in mid-September, with minimum temperatures reported as low as −6.5°C.
The extended duration and frequency of these events contributed to significant agricultural impact.

title: South Australia Spring Frosts (2024)
text:
Spatial definition
This event is defined over major cropping regions of South Australia, particularly the Eyre Peninsula and Mid North, where the most severe impacts were reported.
A bounding box is used to represent the affected region for analysis with gridded datasets such as ERA5.

title: South Australia Spring Frosts (2024)
text:
Impact
The frost events affected a wide range of crops, including wheat, lentils, barley, and canola. Many crops were at flowering or early grain development stages when frost occurred, increasing sensitivity.
Reports indicate substantial yield losses, with some regions experiencing severe damage requiring crops to be cut for hay or abandoned.

title: South Australia Spring Frosts (2024)
text:
Notes and uncertainty
The spatial boundary is approximate and intended for regional-scale analysis.
Localised frost severity likely varied depending on topography and soil type.
This event represents a compound stress scenario, with frost occurring alongside drought conditions.
